title: Enable registration with a backend form

requirements.text: Add a backend registration system that accepts player name and phone number, stores registrations in stable memory, and exposes a register() update method and a getRegistrations() query method.
acceptanceCriteria: A register(name: Text, phone: Text) update method exists and persists the entry in stable storage.
A getRegistrations() query method returns all registered entries.
Duplicate phone numbers are rejected with an error message.

requirements.text: Replace the current WhatsApp link in RegisterButton.tsx with a modal registration form that collects the player's name and phone number and submits them to the backend register() method. Show a success message on successful registration and an error message on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Register Now!' opens a modal with Name and Phone Number input fields.
Submitting the form calls the backend register() method.
On success, the modal shows a confirmation message such as 'Registration successful! 🎉'.
On error (e.g., duplicate phone), an appropriate error message is shown inside the modal.
The modal can be closed without submitting.
The Register Now button retains its existing active shimmer and animation styling.

constraints: Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths).
Keep all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture).
nonGoals: Connecting registrations to Google Sheets or any external service.
Email or SMS notifications for registrations.
An admin dashboard to view registrations beyond the backend query method.
